title: Ipomopsis congesta
taxonomy: Ipomopsis congesta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). It is a member of the genus Ipomopsis, which is a group of plants native to North America. Ipomopsis congesta is further classified as a dicotyledonous perennial plant, meaning it has two seed leaves and grows back year after year. It belongs to the tribe Oenotherae within the Onagraceae family. The exact classification of Ipomopsis congesta can also be represented in binomial nomenclature as a species within the Onagraceae family.

intro: Ipomopsis congesta, also known as the spotted globe-mallow, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall immune function. Bioactive constituents of Ipomopsis congesta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is sometimes used in traditional herbal medicine to support respiratory health and relieve symptoms of coughs and colds.
